title: Artemis in Sierra
content:
В В В В Dramatis PersonпїЅ

В В В В Poet. Philosopher. Jones of Mariposa.



Poet
В В В В Halt! Here we are. Now wheel your mare a trifle
В В В В Just where you stand; then doff your hat and swear
В В В В Never yet was scene you might cover with your rifle
В В В В Half as complete or as marvelously fair.

Philosopher
В В В В Dropped from Olympus or lifted out of Tempe,
В В В В Swung like a censer betwixt the earth and sky!
В В В В He who in Greece sang of flocks and flax and hemp, he
В В В В Here might recall them six thousand feet on high!

Poet
В В В В Well you may say so. The clamor of the river,
В В В В Hum of base toil, and manпїЅs ignoble strife,
В В В В Halt far below, where the stifling sunbeams quiver,
В В В В But never climb to this purer, higher life!

В В В В Not to this glade, where Jones of Mariposa,
В В В В Simple and meek as his flocks weпїЅre looking at,
В В В В Tends his soft charge; nor where his daughter Rosa
В В В В (A shot.)
В В В В Hallo! WhatпїЅs that?

Philosopher
В В В В A something throпїЅ my hat
В В В В Bullet, I think. You were speaking of his daughter?

Poet
В В В В Yes; but your hat you were moving through the leaves;
В В В В Likely he thought it some eagle bent on slaughter.
В В В В Lightly he shoots (A second shot.)

Philosopher
В В В В As one readily perceives.
В В В В Still, he improves! This time your hat has got it,
В В В В Quite near the band! Eh? Oh, just as you please
В В В В Stop, or go on.

Poet
В В В В Perhaps weпїЅd better trot it
В В В В Down through the hollow, and up among the trees.

Both
В В В В Trot, trot, trot, where the bullets cannot follow;
В В В В Trot down and up again among the laurel trees.

Philosopher
В В В В Thanks, that is better; now of this shot-dispensing
В В В В Jones and his girl you were saying

Poet
В В В В Well, you see
В В В В I hang it all! Oh! whatпїЅs the use of fencing!
В В В В Sir, I confess it! these shots were meant for me.

Philosopher
В В В В Are you mad!

Poet
В В В В God knows, I shouldnпїЅt wonder!
В В В В I love this coy nymph, who, coldly as yon peak
В В В В Shines on the river it feeds, yet keeps asunder
В В В В Long have I worshiped, but never dared to speak.

В В В В Till she, no doubt, her love no longer hiding,
В В В В Waked by some chance word her fatherпїЅs jealousy;
В В В В Slips her disdain as an avalanche down gliding
В В В В Sweeps flocks and kin away to clear a path for me.

В В В В Hence his attack.

Philosopher
В В В В I see. What I admire
В В В В Chiefly, I think, in your idyl, so to speak,
В В В В Is the cool modesty that checks your youthful fire,
В В В В Absence of self-love and abstinence of cheek!

В В В В Still, I might mention, IпїЅve met the gentle Rosa,
В В В В Danced with her thrice, to her fatherпїЅs jealous dread;
В В В В And, it is possible, sheпїЅs happened to disclose a
В В В В Ahem! You can fancy why he shoots at me instead.

Poet
В В В В You?

Philosopher
В В В В Me. But kindly take your hand from your revolver,
В В В В I am not choleric but accidents may chance.
В В В В And hereпїЅs the father, who alone can be the solver
В В В В Of this twin riddle of the hat and the romance.

В В В В [Enter Jones of Mariposa.]

Poet
В В В В Speak, shepherd mine!

Philosopher
В В В В Hail! Time-and-cartridge waster,
В В В В Aimless exploder of theories and skill!
В В В В Whom do you shoot?

Jones of Mariposa
В В В В Well, shootinпїЅ ainпїЅt my taste, or
В В В В Ef I shoot anything I only shoot to kill.

В В В В That ainпїЅt whatпїЅs up. I only kem to tell ye
В В В В SportinпїЅ or courtinпїЅ trot homeward for your life!
В В В В Gals will be gals, and pпїЅrпїЅaps itпїЅs just ez well ye
В В В В Larned there was one had no wish to be a wife.

Poet
В В В В What?

Philosopher
В В В В Is this true?

Jones of Mariposa
В В В В I reckon it looks like it.
В В В В She saw ye cominпїЅ. My gun was standinпїЅ by;
В В В В She made a grab, and пїЅfore I up could strike it,
В В В В Blazed at ye both! The critter is so shy!

Poet
В В В В Who?

Jones of Mariposa
В В В В My darter!

Philosopher
В В В В Rosa?

Jones of Mariposa
В В В В  Same! Good-by!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Bret Harte (Francis) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Bret Harte (Francis)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.
